# terrible_community_zabbix_zabbix_action (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of the action

### Optional

- `acknowledge_operations` (String) List of acknowledge operations. Action acknowledge operations are known as update operations since Zabbix 4.0. `Suboptions` are the same as for *operations*.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `conditions` (String) List of conditions to use for filtering results. For more information about suboptions of this option please check out Zabbix API documentation https://www.zabbix.com/documentation/5.0/manual/api/reference/action/object#action_filter_condition
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `esc_period` (String) Default operation step duration. Must be greater than 60 seconds. Accepts seconds, time unit with suffix and user macro since => Zabbix 3.4 Required when `state=present`.
- `eval_type` (String) Filter condition evaluation method. Defaults to `andor` if conditions are less then 2 or if *formula* is not specified. Defaults to `custom_expression` when formula is specified.
- `event_source` (String) Type of events that the action will handle. Required when `state=present`.
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `formula` (String) User-defined expression to be used for evaluating conditions with a custom expression. The expression must contain IDs that reference each condition by its formulaid. The IDs used in the expression must exactly match the ones defined in the *conditions*. No condition can remain unused or omitted. Required when *eval_type=custom_expression*. Use sequential IDs that start at "A". If non-sequential IDs are used, Zabbix re-indexes them. This makes each module run notice the difference in IDs and update the action.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `notify_if_canceled` (Boolean) Weather to notify when escalation is canceled. Can be used when *event_source=trigger*.
- `operations` (String) List of action operations
- `pause_in_maintenance` (Boolean) Whether to pause escalation during maintenance periods or not. Can be used when *event_source=trigger*.
- `pause_symptoms` (Boolean) Whether to pause escalation if event is a symptom event. *supported* if `event_source` is set to `trigger` Works only with >= Zabbix 7.0
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `recovery_operations` (String) List of recovery operations. `Suboptions` are the same as for *operations*.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) State of the action. On `present`, it will create an action if it does not exist or update the action if the associated data is different. On `absent`, it will remove the action if it exists.
- `status` (String) Status of the action.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `msg` (String) The result of the operation
